summary: Iteration 15 — narrow static validation of .github/workflows/auto-tag.yml after adding 'main-v2' to on.push.branches, plus a 2-call backend smoke. All 8 pytest checks green (8/8 = 100%). Workflow file is valid YAML; branches set is exactly {main, master, main-v2}; on.push.paths still contains 'backend/VERSION.txt'; job tag-and-release retains runs-on=ubuntu-latest + contents:write + the three required steps (Read version, Skip if tag already exists, Create and push tag). release.yml confirmed unchanged: tag-triggered on v32.*/v33.*, no branches filter (branch-agnostic). Backend smoke healthy: GET /api/ -> 200, GET /api/native/files -> 200 with valid JSON body. Nothing collateral broken by the workflow edit.
critical_code_review_comments: auto-tag.yml is clean and idempotent (Skip-if-tag-exists guards re-pushes). The 'main-v2' addition is a minimal, surgical change — no semantic drift from the prior main/master behavior.

Once the team fully cuts over to main-v2, consider removing 'main' from the branches list to avoid accidental double-tagging if main ever receives a stray VERSION.txt edit. Non-blocking; safe to defer.

success_rate.frontend: not tested (out of scope per request)

test_credentials: n/a — none required for this scope
context_for_next_testing_agent: Workflow trigger for auto-tag now fires on main, master, AND main-v2. release.yml stays tag-driven (v32.*/v33.*) and is branch-agnostic — do NOT add branches to it. iteration_14 covered the full post-merge backend regression (25/25). For any future workflow-only edits, /app/backend/tests/test_autotag_workflow_iter15.py is the right regression file to extend — it uses the pyyaml `on`->True quirk handler (cfg.get('on') or cfg.get(True)).
rca of the issue: n/a — no defect found. Edit was a one-line additive change to on.push.branches; static parse + structural assertions confirm correctness, and the backend was untouched.
